display.purpose: Hero and section statements in Bricolage Grotesque black. Loudest element on any tile.

body.purpose: Bios and project copy in Instrument Sans. Cap measure 65-75ch.

label.purpose: Uppercase 0.3em tracked kickers and metadata tags. <=4 words, used sparingly.

mono.purpose: System monospace for dates, periods, and footer only.

shadows.name: card-lift
shadows.purpose: Hover lift on an interactive tile. Pairs with a scale-to-0.98 press.

shadows.name: floating-nav
shadows.purpose: Persistent lift on the glass timeline sidebar.

shadows.name: node-glow
shadows.purpose: White halo around the active nav node. A status light, not a drop shadow.

shadows.name: pill-lift
shadows.purpose: Slight lift on white tech-stack pills over the blue Hero fill.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Default content reveal (fadeInUp). Exponential ease-out, no bounce.

motion.name: press
motion.purpose: Tactile press feedback on every clickable card. The 'playable' signature.

motion.name: marquee
motion.purpose: Tech-stack and Work background marquees. Pausable. Needs a reduced-motion stop.

motion.name: layout-shared
motion.purpose: Shared-layout card expand/collapse. The core 'operate the interface' interaction.

narrative.overview: This is not a page you read; it is an interface you operate. The whole system is a dark canvas holding a grid of saturated, solid-color tiles, and every tile is a control: click one and it expands in place, revealing the content within. The playfulness is structural, not cosmetic. The personality is playful, energetic, and human, carried by oversized Bricolage Grotesque headlines, a small set of committed high-saturation card colors against pure black, and a constant film-grain texture. Depth is deliberately understated: tiles sit flat at rest and only lift, glow, or shadow in response to interaction. What this system rejects is overdesigned, gimmicky decoration; the line to hold is playful, never gimmicky.
keyCharacteristics: A dark stage (pure black #000000) holding a bento grid of committed-color tiles.
One signature primitive, the tactile click-to-expand BentoCard, reused as the whole architecture.
Oversized grotesque display type as the loudest voice; color second; motion third.
Flat surfaces; depth appears only on interaction (hover-lift, glow halo, grain).
A balanced dev-and-designer identity, shown through range, not asserted in copy.

rules.name: The One Loud Blue Rule
rules.body: Electric Ultramarine (#2512F5) appears as a fill, never as a hairline or tiny accent. Glow Blue (#60A5FA) is the only blue allowed to act as a thin accent or glow; keep the two roles distinct.

rules.name: The Black-Ink-on-Lemon Rule
rules.body: Text on the Pale Lemon tile is always pure black (#000000). Never grey text on the yellow; it washes out and fails contrast.

rules.name: The Loud-Display Rule
rules.body: Headlines are the loudest element on any tile: black weight, tight tracking, set big. Hierarchy comes from scale and weight contrast, not color shifts or underlines.

rules.name: The Flat-At-Rest Rule
rules.body: Nothing carries a shadow until it is touched, active, or floating above the grid. Depth is feedback, not decoration.

rules.name: The Grain Rule
rules.body: The 3% film-grain overlay is non-negotiable and global. It keeps flat saturated color from looking like a default Tailwind block.

dos: Do keep depth as feedback: flat at rest, shadow/glow only on hover, active, or float.
Do keep the global 3% film grain on every screen; it's what makes flat color feel made, not defaulted.
Do let one Electric Ultramarine fill (#2512F5) dominate and keep Glow Blue (#60A5FA) as the only thin/glow blue.
Do set black text on the Pale Lemon tile, always.
Do drive hierarchy with scale + weight contrast in Bricolage Grotesque, not with extra color or ornament.
Do keep whileTap/whileHover press feedback on anything clickable; the tactility is the brand's playable promise.
Do keep body text on dark tiles at #D1D5DB or brighter, and verify 4.5:1; bump toward white before going fainter.
Do give every animation a prefers-reduced-motion: reduce fallback; the marquee, rotating Hero shapes, and layout transitions currently lack one.
donts: Don't add overdesigned, gimmicky decoration. No effect for its own sake; if a flourish doesn't clarify or delight in service of the work, cut it. Playful, never gimmicky.
Don't stack a tiny uppercase tracked kicker above every section; treat it as an occasional accent, not default scaffolding.
Don't give tiles a resting shadow or ambient glow; depth that isn't a response to interaction is wrong here.
Don't use grey or low-opacity text on the Lemon or Ultramarine fills; use black, white, or a transparency of the foreground color.
Don't introduce a fourth display typeface or a second sans. Bricolage + Instrument + system mono is the cap.
Don't let oversized headlines overflow their tile on tablet/mobile; test real copy at every breakpoint and reduce the clamp before it clips.
Don't use background-clip:text gradient headings, glassmorphism beyond the one floating nav, or border-left colored stripes.
